# Let's open the file and print its contents and repr to inspect bytes and indices.
path = "/mnt/data/file-6jqaXp1bxooywJWA26AxBm-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "rb") as f:
    data_bytes = f.read()
data = data_bytes.decode('utf-8')
len(data), data[:1000]

text = data
# find all double-quoted spans
import re
quote_spans = [(m.start(), m.end(), m.group(1)) for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text)]
len(quote_spans), quote_spans[:20]

for start_quote, end_quote, inner in quote_spans:
    # start_quote is index of starting double quote char
    inner_start = start_quote+1
    inner_end = end_quote-1
    context_start = max(0, inner_start-64)
    context_end = min(len(text), inner_end+64)
    context = text[context_start:context_end]
    print("----")
    print("Inner:", inner)
    print("Indices inner_start, inner_end:", inner_start, inner_end)
    print("Context:", repr(context))
print(text)

# find all occurrences of '{' and print some context around them
for m in re.finditer(r'\{[^}]*\}', text):
    s = m.start()
    e = m.end()
    context = text[max(0, s-120):min(len(text), e+20)]
    print("----")
    print("Brace block:", text[s:e])
    print("Context:", context)

for m in re.finditer(r'﴿[^﴾]*﴾', text):
    s = m.start()
    e = m.end()
    print("Found bracket: ", text[s:e], " at ", s, e)
print("Index of 'فَبِمَا أَغْوَيْتَنِي' :", text.find("فَبِمَا أَغْوَيْتَنِي"))

# Display substring around the index to see the pattern
i = text.find("فَبِمَا أَغْوَيْتَنِي")
print(text[max(0,i-100):i+200])

text = data

# trimming function per rule: trim ONLY edge characters if present at span edges:
import unicodedata

trim_chars = set([
    ' ', '\t', '\n', '\r', '\x0b', '\x0c', # whitespace
    '"', "'", '«', '»', '“', '”', '‘', '’', # quotes
    '(', ')', '[', ']', '{', '}', '<', '>', # brackets
    '،', '؛', ':', '.', ',', '!', '?', '…', ';', # punctuation (includes semicolon ascii)
    'ـ' # tatweel
])
# Also include Arabic dash? but good.

def trim_edges(s):
    # remove leading/trailing characters in trim_chars repeatedly
    start = 0
    end = len(s)
    while start < end and s[start] in trim_chars:
        start += 1
    while end > start and s[end-1] in trim_chars:
        end -= 1
    return s[start:end], start, end

# Helper to ensure substring matches raw at computed start index and handle sliding up to 2 chars inward if necessary
def find_and_assert_span(span_text, search_start=0, search_end=None):
    """
    Find an occurrence of span_text between search_start and search_end.
    Returns start index if found. If not found, raises ValueError.
    """
    if search_end is None:
        search_end = len(text)
    idx = text.find(span_text, search_start, search_end)
    if idx == -1:
        # try searching anywhere
        idx = text.find(span_text)
        if idx == -1:
            raise ValueError(f"span_text not found: {repr(span_text[:30])}...")
    # assert match
    if text[idx:idx+len(span_text)] != span_text:
        raise AssertionError("Mismatch after find")
    return idx

results = []  # list of dicts with start,end,text,type

# 1. Double-quoted spans (we already have them)
import re
for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text):
    inner = m.group(1)
    inner_start = m.start(1)
    inner_end = m.end(1)
    # trim only edges per rule
    trimmed, off_start, off_end = trim_edges(inner)
    # trimmed is text without leading/trailing trim chars; but off_start/off_end are offsets inside inner
    # Map trimmed absolute indices
    abs_start = inner_start + off_start
    abs_end = inner_start + off_end
    # verify raw[abs_start:abs_end] equals trimmed
    assert text[abs_start:abs_end] == trimmed
    # determine citation type from context - look at ±64 chars for cues
    ctx = text[max(0, abs_start-64):min(len(text), abs_end+64)]
    # check hadith cues
    hadith_cues = ["قال رسول", "قال رسول الله", "قال النبي", "قال رسول الله صلى", "في الحديث", "رواه", "روى", "حدثنا", "عن النبي"]
    ayah_cues = ["قال الله", "قال تعالى", "يقول سبحانه", "يقول الله", "حيث يقول", "كما قال", "كما قال تعالى", "قال الله تعالى", "قال تعالى", "قال سبحانه", "الآية", "في القرآن", "قال"]
    # classify by presence of hadith cues within ±64 context; prefer hadith cues if found.
    ctype = None
    for hc in hadith_cues:
        if hc in ctx:
            ctype = "Hadeeth"
            break
    if ctype is None:
        # check ayah cues (only if hadith not found)
        for ac in ayah_cues:
            if ac in ctx:
                ctype = "Aya"
                break
    if ctype is None:
        # fallback use 'Aya' if text contains Arabic tashkeel maybe; but safe fallback is Aya
        ctype = "Aya"
    results.append({"start": abs_start, "end": abs_end, "text": trimmed, "type": ctype, "source":"double-quote"})
    
results, len(results)

for r in results:
    print("----")
    print("span text:", r["text"])
    ctx = text[max(0, r["start"]-64):min(len(text), r["end"]+64)]
    print("context:", ctx)
    found_h = [hc for hc in ["قال رسول", "قال رسول الله", "قال النبي", "قال رسول الله صلى", "في الحديث", "رواه", "روى", "حدثنا", "عن النبي"] if hc in ctx]
    found_a = [ac for ac in ["قال الله", "قال تعالى", "يقول سبحانه", "يقول الله", "حيث يقول", "كما قال", "كما قال تعالى", "قال الله تعالى", "قال تعالى", "قال سبحانه", "الآية", "في القرآن", "قال"] if ac in ctx]
    print("hadith cues:", found_h)
    print("ayah cues:", found_a)

def classify_span_by_context(span_start, span_end):
    ctx_start = max(0, span_start-64)
    ctx_end = min(len(text), span_end+64)
    ctx = text[ctx_start:ctx_end]
    # check bracket patterns for ayah
    if re.search(r'\[[^\]]*:\s*\d', ctx) or re.search(r'\{[^}]*:\s*\d', ctx) or re.search(r'﴿[^﴾]*:[0-9٠١٢٣٤٥٦٧٨٩]+﴾', ctx):
        return "Aya"
    # hadith cues
    hadith_cues = ["قال رسول", "قال رسول الله", "قال النبي", "قال رسول الله صلى", "في الحديث", "رواه", "روى", "حدثنا", "عن النبي"]
    for hc in hadith_cues:
        if hc in ctx:
            return "Hadeeth"
    # ayah cues
    ayah_cues = ["قال الله", "قال تعالى", "يقول سبحانه", "يقول الله", "حيث يقول", "كما قال", "كما قال تعالى", "قال الله تعالى", "قال تعالى", "قال سبحانه", "الآية", "في القرآن"]
    for ac in ayah_cues:
        if ac in ctx:
            return "Aya"
    # fallback: if the span contains '*' or Arabic diacritics or uppercase forms it likely is Ayah
    return "Aya"

results = []
for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text):
    inner = m.group(1)
    inner_start = m.start(1)
    inner_end = m.end(1)
    trimmed, off_start, off_end = trim_edges(inner)
    abs_start = inner_start + off_start
    abs_end = inner_start + off_end
    ctype = classify_span_by_context(abs_start, abs_end)
    results.append({"start": abs_start, "end": abs_end, "text": trimmed, "type": ctype, "source":"double-quote"})
results

# process brace matches {..}
for m in re.finditer(r'\{[^}]*\}', text):
    bstart = m.start()
    bend = m.end()
    # Look back up to 300 chars for a colon
    lookback = 300
    search_start = max(0, bstart - lookback)
    slice_ = text[search_start:bstart]
    colon_pos_in_slice = slice_.rfind(":")
    if colon_pos_in_slice == -1:
        # try Arabic colon? but likely present
        colon_pos_in_slice = slice_.rfind("：")
    if colon_pos_in_slice != -1:
        abs_colon = search_start + colon_pos_in_slice
        block_start = abs_colon + 1
    else:
        # fallback: find last occurrence of newline
        nl = slice_.rfind("\n")
        if nl != -1:
            block_start = search_start + nl + 1
        else:
            block_start = search_start
    block_text = text[block_start:bstart]
    # split on '*' to separate verses
    parts = [p for p in block_text.split('*') if p.strip()!='']
    # For each part find its absolute start within [block_start, bstart)
    for p in parts:
        trimmed, off_s, off_e = trim_edges(p)
        if len(trimmed) < 10:
            continue
        # find absolute occurrence of p within block region
        # Use find on trimmed text to avoid leading/trailing punctuation that were trimmed earlier
        try:
            # find trimmed within that block
            abs_p_start = text.find(trimmed, block_start, bstart)
            if abs_p_start == -1:
                # maybe trimmed contains special unicode that different? fallback to p
                abs_p_start = text.find(p.strip(), block_start, bstart)
                if abs_p_start == -1:
                    # try to search globally
                    abs_p_start = text.find(trimmed)
                    if abs_p_start == -1:
                        print("Warning: could not find trimmed part in block:", repr(trimmed))
                        continue
            abs_p_end = abs_p_start + len(trimmed)
            # check overlap with existing results
            overlap = False
            for rr in results:
                if not (abs_p_end <= rr["start"] or abs_p_start >= rr["end"]):
                    overlap = True
                    break
            if overlap:
                # allow duplicate occurrences at different positions but not overlapping with any already captured span?
                # If overlap occurs it likely means we already captured this exact region via double-quote earlier.
                # skip to avoid nested duplicates
                continue
            results.append({"start": abs_p_start, "end": abs_p_end, "text": trimmed, "type": "Aya", "source":"brace"})
        except Exception as e:
            print("Exception processing brace part:", repr(p), e)

len(results)

# process special Arabic parentheses '﴿ ... ﴾'
for m in re.finditer(r'﴿[^﴾]*﴾', text):
    bs = m.start()
    be = m.end()
    # find last colon within 300 chars
    search_start = max(0, bs-300)
    slice_ = text[search_start:bs]
    colon_pos = slice_.rfind(":")
    if colon_pos != -1:
        abs_colon = search_start + colon_pos
        block_start = abs_colon + 1
    else:
        nl = slice_.rfind("\n")
        if nl != -1:
            block_start = search_start + nl + 1
        else:
            block_start = search_start
    block_text = text[block_start:bs]
    # block_text may contain verse before the special bracket
    if block_text.strip()=="":
        continue
    # possibly multiple verses separated by '*'?
    parts = [p for p in block_text.split('*') if p.strip()!='']
    for p in parts:
        trimmed, off_s, off_e = trim_edges(p)
        if len(trimmed) < 10:
            continue
        # find trimmed within that area
        abs_p_start = text.find(trimmed, block_start, bs)
        if abs_p_start == -1:
            abs_p_start = text.find(trimmed)
            if abs_p_start == -1:
                print("Could not find trimmed in special parens:", repr(trimmed))
                continue
        abs_p_end = abs_p_start + len(trimmed)
        # check overlap with existing results
        overlap = any(not (abs_p_end <= rr["start"] or abs_p_start >= rr["end"]) for rr in results)
        if overlap:
            continue
        results.append({"start": abs_p_start, "end": abs_p_end, "text": trimmed, "type":"Aya", "source":"special-paren"})
        
# Now sort
results_sorted = sorted(results, key=lambda x: x["start"])
for r in results_sorted:
    print(r["start"], r["end"], r["type"], repr(r["text"])[:200], "source:", r.get("source",""))

# colon-based detection
ayah_cues_list = ["قال الله", "قال تعالى", "يقول سبحانه", "يقول الله", "حيث يقول", "كما قال", "كما قال تعالى", "قال الله تعالى", "قال تعالى", "قال سبحانه", "الآية", "في القرآن", "كما قال أيضا", "كما قال تعالى أيضا", "حيث يقول سبحانه"]
hadith_cues_list = ["قال رسول", "قال رسول الله", "قال النبي", "قال رسول الله صلى", "في الحديث", "رواه", "روى", "حدثنا", "عن النبي", "قال الرسول", "قال صلى الله عليه وسلم"]

colon_positions = [m.start() for m in re.finditer(r':', text)]
added = 0
for ci in colon_positions:
    prev_ctx = text[max(0, ci-64):ci]
    # check for cues
    is_ayah_cue = any(c in prev_ctx for c in ayah_cues_list)
    is_hadith_cue = any(c in prev_ctx for c in hadith_cues_list)
    if not (is_ayah_cue or is_hadith_cue):
        continue
    # find next boundary among candidates
    search_end = min(len(text), ci+400)
    # find earliest occurrence of any delimiter
    possible_delims = ['{','﴾','"','[','\n','.','،',')','?','!']
    next_positions = [text.find(d, ci+1, search_end) for d in possible_delims]
    # remove -1
    next_positions = [p for p in next_positions if p!=-1]
    if next_positions:
        boundary = min(next_positions)
    else:
        boundary = search_end
    candidate = text[ci+1:boundary]
    trimmed, off_s, off_e = trim_edges(candidate)
    if len(trimmed) < 10:
        continue
    # find absolute start of trimmed within [ci+1, boundary]
    abs_p_start = text.find(trimmed, ci+1, boundary)
    if abs_p_start == -1:
        # try global
        abs_p_start = text.find(trimmed)
    if abs_p_start == -1:
        continue
    abs_p_end = abs_p_start + len(trimmed)
    # ensure not overlapping with existing results
    overlap = any(not (abs_p_end <= rr["start"] or abs_p_start >= rr["end"]) for rr in results)
    if overlap:
        continue
    # classify: if hadith cue present and no bracket, classify Hadeeth; otherwise Aya
    # also check bracket presence in +/-64 region
    ctx = text[max(0, abs_p_start-64):min(len(text), abs_p_end+64)]
    ctype = "Aya"
    # check for bracket pattern
    if re.search(r'\[[^\]]*:\s*\d', ctx) or re.search(r'\{[^}]*:\s*\d', ctx) or re.search(r'﴿[^﴾]*:[0-9٠١٢٣٤٥٦٧٨٩]+﴾', ctx):
        ctype = "Aya"
    elif is_hadith_cue and not is_ayah_cue:
        ctype = "Hadeeth"
    elif is_ayah_cue:
        ctype = "Aya"
    results.append({"start": abs_p_start, "end": abs_p_end, "text": trimmed, "type": ctype, "source":"colon"})
    added += 1

added, len(results)

# print around index 2600-2700
print(text[2600:2720])

# filter out colon-based results that are generic 'الحمد لله...' introductions
filtered_results = []
for r in results:
    if r.get("source") == "colon" and r["text"].startswith("الحمد لله"):
        # drop
        continue
    filtered_results.append(r)
results = filtered_results
# sort and print again
results_sorted = sorted(results, key=lambda x: x["start"])
for r in results_sorted:
    print(r["start"], r["end"], r["type"], repr(r["text"])[:200], "source:", r.get("source",""))

# Validate all results: substring matches
for r in results_sorted:
    s = r["start"]; e = r["end"]; span = r["text"]
    if text[s:e] != span:
        print("Mismatch for span:", r)
        print("raw slice:", repr(text[s:e]))
        raise SystemExit("Mismatch")
print("All spans validated: text[s:e] equals span_text for each.")

يَشَاءُ","citation_type":"Aya"},{"span_start":5035,"span_end":5175,"span_text":"فَإِنَّ اللهَ يُضِلُّ مَنْ يَشَاءُ وَيَهْدِي مَنْ يَشَاءُ فَلَا تَذْهَبْ نَفْسُكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسَرَاتٍ إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ بِمَا يَصْنَعُونَ","citation_type":"Aya"},{"span_start":5201,"span_end":5280,"span_text":"مَنْ يَشَأِ اللهُ يُضْلِلْهُ وَمَنْ يَشَأْ يَجْعَلْهُ عَلَى صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ","citation_type":"Aya"},{"span_start":5447,"span_end":5485,"span_text":"وَمَا يُضِلُّ بِهِ إِلَّا الفَاسِقِينَ","citation_type":"Aya"},{"span_start":5524,"span_end":5588,"span_text":"إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ كَانَ مِنَ الجِنِّ فَفَسَقَ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِ","citation_type":"Aya"}]}
